Lesson 4

SPEAKING &
SOFT SKILLS

Introducing yourself and talking about your job

BOOK RECOMMENDATION

Soft Skills: The Software Developer's Life Manual
by John Sonmez.

What is an elevator pitch?

An elevator pitch is a quick overview of your background and skills or a brief description of any products or services you want to offer. An elevator pitch is called so because it shouldn’t last longer than a typical elevator ride: 30 seconds to 1 minute. After all, you don’t want to bore your listeners, do you?

Where can you use an elevator pitch?

  • At job fairs and networking events
  • At interviews as an answer to “Tell me about yourself”
  • In your LinkedIn “About” section
  • On your website or blog
  • When you introduce yourself to a new client or team
Listening practice

Listen again and complete the dialogues.

Type the missing words in the gaps. Then check your answers.

Dialogue 1 Track 1.3

A: Nice to meet you, Lisa! What do you do?

B: I work robotics. I’m a design engineer.

A: Oh, wow! What is that?

B: Well, my job is to design how the robot is going to look. I also that all the technical components we are using will actually work when we put them together.

A: That sounds really exciting!

B: It is! Anyway, what do you do ?

Dialogue 2 Track 1.4

A: So, you’re a freelancer, huh?

B: Yeah, I’m a . I mostly work on my own. On an average day, I get a new project from a client, estimate how much time it will take, of the design, code it up and send it to the client.

A: It’s probably a fun job.

B: What I like about it is that I can do a really of projects. For example, right now I’m working on an e-commerce website for a pretty big company and next week I’m going to work on a blog for a fashion designer!

Dialogue 3 Track 1.5

A: So, your sister tells me you own a business?

B: Yes, I that helps developers and business owners meet and network with each other. We have 2000 users right now. 400 of them found a job in the last three months and 80 of them are now building their own startups!

A: Really! That’s amazing!

B: Thank you! So what about you? Where do you work?

A: I actually work a small web agency as a back-end developer. I can full-stack development as well and, in fact, I’m currently looking for a full-stack developer job.

B: Sounds like you could use my platform!

A: Totally! What’s it called by the way?

Useful phrases

Say what you do professionally.

Use these phrases in your own introduction. The examples are general, not IT-specific.

I work in...
I work in recruitment / accounting / customer service / education / sales.
I work as a...
I work as a recruiter at a staffing agency.
I work at / for...
I work for an international logistics company.
I help...
I help clients choose the right insurance plan.
I take care of / I handle...
I handle monthly financial reports and invoices.
My job is to...
My job is to support customers and solve their problems.
I make sure that...
I make sure that candidates have a smooth hiring experience.
I oversee / I’m in charge of...
I’m in charge of employee training and onboarding.
Right now I am...
Right now I am working on a new client project.
Pitch builder

Create your own elevator pitch.

Answer the questions. Your final pitch will follow the same three-step structure from the model above.

1. Who you are and what you do

2. Identify a problem and explain how you solve it

3. Wrap up with a call to action

Your elevator pitch will appear here.
Keep the conversation going
  • What do you enjoy most about your job?
  • How did you get interested in this field?
  • What has been your biggest challenge so far?
  • What achievement are you most proud of?
  • What would you like to learn next?
  • If you could change one thing about your work, what would it be?
  • What kind of people do you usually work with?
  • Where do you see yourself in five years?
Pitch examples

Choose a situation and adapt your pitch.

Open a card, read the model, and type your own ideas into the grey blanks.

30-Second Elevator Pitch

A short pitch for a first meeting. It gives just enough information to make people interested.

Did you know that of the waste produced by comes from ? That’s . We think this is a serious problem, which is why we’ve made a commitment to . In just the last , we’ve increased from to .
Keep the conversation going
  • Can you tell me more about this problem?
  • Why is this issue important to you?
  • Have you already helped someone with this?
  • What results have you achieved?
  • What would happen if this problem wasn't solved?
  • What makes your solution different?
Two-Minute Interview Pitch

A longer interview answer with introduction, background, goals, and plan.

I am a with more than of professional experience, expertise in , and a proven track record of .

While I was , I . For the last , I’ve been working at , where I’ve .

Now that I’ve grown more confident as a , I want to apply my skills and experience to . I’m particularly interested in .

I’ve been following the exciting work that your company is doing in . I think, with my years of experience, I could help you .
Keep the conversation going
  • Which experience taught you the most?
  • What was the biggest challenge in your career?
  • Which project are you most proud of?
  • How have your goals changed over time?
  • What skills would you still like to develop?
  • Why do you think this company is a good fit for you?
Job Interview Pitch

Use this when the interviewer says: “Tell me about yourself.”

My name is , and I’m a with of experience at . I specialize in , and I’m looking to join where I will be able to .
Keep the conversation going
  • Why did you choose this profession?
  • What do you enjoy most about your work?
  • What kind of working environment do you prefer?
  • What's one thing your colleagues usually appreciate about you?
  • What motivates you every day?
Networking Event Pitch

A friendly pitch for job fairs, conferences, meetups, or professional events.

You might not know my name yet, but you are likely familiar with . I have helped .
Keep the conversation going
  • What inspired you to start doing this?
  • What projects are you working on now?
  • What kind of people would you like to meet?
  • Is there anyone you'd like to collaborate with?
  • What are you hoping to learn from this event?
Formal Meeting Pitch

A polished pitch for a client, employer, manager, or business meeting.

My name is , and I am a . I can provide you that . Would you like to be seen as the solution to ?
Keep the conversation going
  • What do you usually help clients with?
  • How do you understand a client's needs?
  • Can you describe a successful project?
  • What makes your approach different?
  • How do you build trust with new clients?
Sales Pitch

Use this to identify a pain point and offer a practical solution.

I understand that you’re having trouble with . That can be so frustrating, especially in a company that . We have a highly trained staff that can help with minimal interruption to . Can you tell me what you’re most concerned about with ?
Keep the conversation going
  • What problems do your customers usually have?
  • Which solution works best?
  • What questions do customers ask most often?
  • How do you know your solution is successful?
  • What advice would you give someone with this problem?
A Customer Story Pitch Example

Use this when you want to show proof through a client story.

We have a client with a , similar to yours. Before they started working with us, they weren’t . In the last , they’ve started and increased by .
Keep the conversation going
  • What was the customer's biggest challenge?
  • Why was that situation difficult?
  • Which solution worked best?
  • What did you learn from this experience?
  • Would you do anything differently today?
Elevator Pitch for Internship

Use this when you have limited experience but want to show motivation and direction.

I’m a student at . I want to pursue a career in after I graduate. I have a strong academic record, and I’m looking to expand my experience into . I’ve always been interested in , and I’d love an opportunity to intern at and learn as much as I can.
Keep the conversation going
  • Why are you interested in this internship?
  • What would you like to learn first?
  • Which university project are you most proud of?
  • What skills can you already offer?
  • How will this internship help your future career?
General Business Elevator Pitch

Use this to explain what your business does and why it is useful.

Hi, my name is , and I’m the of . It’s lovely to meet you! I saw that you are . When so much care goes into , it can be hard to also make sure . That’s why we partner with to help them . Can you tell me a little bit about ?
Keep the conversation going
  • How did your business start?
  • What makes your company different?
  • What kind of clients do you enjoy working with?
  • What has been your biggest success so far?
  • What are your future plans?

Designed as an interactive English lesson game for B1–B2 learners.
